title: Studio Decision Log
organization: Two Marshalls Studios — Studio Operating System (TMS-OS)
summary: Preserves major verified studio decisions together with the current WS086 repository reconciliation checkpoint.

sections.title: Purpose
paragraphs: This document is the permanent Studio Decision Log for Two Marshalls Studios. It preserves major verified decisions recovered from the beginning of the project and provides the controlled location for future approved studio decisions.
The log exists so important decisions do not remain only in chat history, temporary notes, screenshots, local files, or one person's memory.

sections.title: Permanent Rule
paragraphs: Major studio decisions must be preserved as permanent records and must not depend on chat history for future recovery.
A decision belongs in this log when it materially affects studio direction, architecture, operating rules, documentation, development practice, access, recovery, or future work.

sections.title: Historical Recovery Rule
items: Historical decisions are recorded only when supported by recovered project evidence or explicit approved direction.
Exact dates are not invented when the recovered evidence does not verify them.
Future ideas are not recorded as completed decisions.
Unapproved improvements remain in ENH-LOG-001 until Stephen approves a path.
When a later verified decision replaces an earlier decision, the earlier record remains preserved and its status is updated rather than deleted.

sections.title: Recovered Baseline Decisions
paragraphs: The following decisions form the verified historical baseline recovered from the project to date.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0001 — Establish Two Marshalls Studios and TMS-OS

rows: Operate Two Marshalls Studios through a central Studio Operating System called TMS-OS.
The studio requires one governed operating foundation for projects, systems, publishing, documentation, and studio operations.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0002 — Use the Studio Portal as the Command Center

rows: Use the Studio Portal as the visible command center for Two Marshalls Studios.
The portal provides a common place to access and present governed studio information.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0003 — Establish StudioDB as the Structured Data Foundation

rows: Move studio information toward governed StudioDB records rather than allowing individual pages or disconnected files to become permanent data authorities.
A shared structured data foundation improves consistency, reuse, search, validation, and long-term control.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0004 — Evolve to Fully Modular StudioDB

rows: Separate studio data responsibilities into modular data files and combine them through the shared studio data model.
The modular architecture reduces dependence on one large data file and supports clearer ownership, maintenance, and growth.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0005 — Use Shared Rendering and Shared Search

rows: Use shared system behavior for supported rendering and search rather than creating isolated page-specific versions.
Shared behavior reduces duplication and allows supported information to work consistently across departments.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0006 — Make Studio Work Accessible from Multiple Workstations

rows: Important Two Marshalls Studios work must be accessible from multiple locations and workstations and must not be limited to one computer.
The studio requires continuity, recoverability, and a clear process for working from more than one location.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0007 — Preserve Source Code and Project History in GitHub

rows: Use GitHub to preserve source code and project history.
Source control protects development history and supports work across computers without making one local workstation the only copy.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0008 — Establish Google Headquarters

rows: Use the approved Google Headquarters structure as the permanent human-readable documentation layer.
Important human-readable studio knowledge must be available outside temporary chat and local files.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0009 — Prevent Competing Sources of Truth

rows: TMS-OS and StudioDB govern structured records; GitHub preserves source code and project history; Google Headquarters preserves permanent human-readable documentation.
Clear system roles prevent temporary files, chat records, screenshots, and duplicate documents from becoming competing permanent authorities.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0010 — Use Controlled Work Sessions

rows: Use a controlled work-session process and preserve the active goal, completed work, tests, decisions, lessons, and next action.
Important work must remain recoverable and transferable between sessions.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0011 — Stop New Work When a Bug Appears

rows: When a bug appears, stop unrelated new work, identify the source, fix the source, and retest before continuing.
This prevents known problems from being buried under new features or unrelated changes.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0012 — Do Not Add Unapproved Enhancements During Build Sessions

rows: Stay strictly focused on the agreed studio vision and current approved work. Do not add unrelated systems or enhancements unless Stephen explicitly approves them.
Foundation work must not be derailed by attractive but unapproved improvements.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0013 — Maintain a Permanent Enhancement Ideas Log

rows: Capture enhancement ideas from the beginning of the project in ENH-LOG-001 and defer them until a controlled review or approved work path.
Ideas should not be lost, but they also should not interrupt approved foundation work.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0014 — Create Permanent Documentation from Completed Work

rows: Completed important work must be converted into structured permanent documentation through the controlled documentation process.
Chat history, temporary notes, screenshots, local files, and a single workstation cannot be the only place important knowledge exists.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0015 — Complete Historical Recovery Before Normal Forward Development

rows: Complete the controlled historical recovery and documentation reconciliation milestone before returning to normal forward development.
The studio must preserve and reconcile important knowledge already created before continuing to build on top of it.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0016 — Recover the 16-Category Documentation Package

rows: Use the recovered 16-category package as the required coverage model for the permanent human-readable documentation layer.
The category model identifies missing permanent knowledge without treating the categories as a simple numbered upload sequence.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0017 — Verify Permanent Documents Before Counting Them

rows: Count a permanent document as verified only after it is placed in its approved Google Headquarters location and visually confirmed.
This prevents temporary uploads, backups, chat records, screenshots, or unverified references from being counted as permanent documentation.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0018 — Use Full Files for Code Changes

rows: When code changes are required, provide the complete updated file rather than partial snippets or fragments.
Complete files reduce placement errors and make changes easier to apply and verify.

sections.title: DEC-2026-0019 — Use Exact Step-by-Step Instructions

rows: Studio build instructions must state exactly where to go, what to click or enter, how to test, and what result to expect without skipping steps.
The process must remain repeatable and easy to follow during active work.

sections.title: Current Decision-Control State
items: Historical baseline decisions: Recovered and established in this document.
Repository reconciliation: In progress during Work Session 086.
Future major decisions: Must be added only after approval.
Enhancement ideas: Continue to be captured in ENH-LOG-001 until approved.
Architecture changes: Update ARCH-001 and ARCH-EVO-001 when applicable.
Current-state effects: Update STATE-001 and DOC-STATE-001 when applicable.

sections.title: Required Fields for Future Decisions
items: Decision ID
Decision title
Decision area
Decision
Reason or operating effect
Source work session or supporting record when available
Status
Related permanent records that require updates

sections.title: Future Decision Statuses
items: Proposed — identified but not approved.
Approved — explicitly accepted for action.
Active / Verified — implemented or established and confirmed.
Superseded — replaced by a later verified decision.
Retired — intentionally no longer active.

sections.title: Decision Update Procedure
items: Identify the major decision.
Confirm that it is approved or historically verified.
Assign the next controlled decision ID.
Record the decision and its operating effect.
Identify every permanent record affected by the decision.
Update those records through PROC-003.
Verify the repository records and any required human-readable documentation.

sections.title: Relationship to Other Permanent Records
items: STATE-001 records the current system checkpoint.
ROADMAP-001 records completed, active, and future direction.
HIST-001 preserves the broader historical sequence.
ARCH-001 describes the current architecture.
ARCH-EVO-001 preserves verified architectural evolution.
ENH-LOG-001 preserves unapproved or deferred enhancement ideas.
PROC-003 controls how completed work and decisions become permanent documentation.

sections.title: Permanent Statement
paragraphs: Two Marshalls Studios will preserve major decisions as governed permanent records so future work can understand not only what exists, but what was deliberately decided and why.
The decision log must continue from the beginning-of-project historical baseline established here and must be updated as future major decisions are approved and verified.

revisionHistory.summary: Connected the verified permanent document to the shared structured Document Template Engine.
Reconciled the decision log with the verified repository governance model.

verification.method: Reconciled against the verified v0.28.1 repository during Work Session 086.
